mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 0.5% signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors rotate into equities seeking yield.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS falls below 0.5% and remains there for 2 consecutive months
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle with near-zero rates
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in total nonfarm payroll employment of more than 200,000 persons signals robust labor market momentum that supports consumer spending and corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 50% of months with PAYEMS growth > 200k, or correlation between PAYEMS monthly change and 5-day forward returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in retail sales and housing starts signals weakening aggregate demand that portends lower corporate earnings and equity valuations. When both RSAFS and HOUST decline month-over-month simultaneously, S&P 500 forward returns turn negative over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines > 1% AND HOUST declines > 2% in the same month
regime_hint: late-cycle contraction or post-shock demand collapse
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of jointly triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient in a logistic regression is not statistically significant (p > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production below 101.0 (the current level) signals supply-side weakness and potential stagflation risk. When INDPRO falls more than 0.5 points month-over-month, S&P 500 forward returns decline over the next 60 trading days as investors reprice earnings and inflation expectations.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines > 0.5 points in a single month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 65% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the unemployment rate above 4.5% signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer confidence and spending power. This triggers a negative feedback loop to corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 58% of instances when UNRATE > 4.5%, or the directional accuracy of the signal is below 52% over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint signal of rising inflation (CPIAUCSL > 280) combined with elevated federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS > 4.0%) indicates a restrictive monetary policy stance that suppresses equity valuations through higher discount rates and reduced real earnings growth, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with elevated inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 62% of jointly triggered instances, or the interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.20).

mechanism_narrative: A quarter-over-quarter increase in real GDP (GDPC1) above 2.5% annualized signals strong economic growth that supports corporate earnings expansion and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days in post-recovery regimes.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 55% of quarters with GDPC1 growth > 2.5%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint expansion in housing starts and retail sales signals strong consumer demand and housing market health. When both HOUST and RSAFS increase month-over-month simultaneously, S&P 500 forward returns turn positive over the next 5 trading days as investors anticipate sustained earnings growth from consumer-facing sectors.
candidate_trigger: HOUST increases > 3% AND RSAFS increases > 1% in the same month
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 58% of jointly triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient in a logistic regression is not statistically significant (p > 0.18).
